message:
test(btree): add B-tree invariant, S3-FIFO, swizzle, and overflow correctness tests

21 new tests covering sorted order after random insert/delete (proptest),
S3-FIFO capacity invariants and ghost queue tracking, SwizzleRegistry
concurrent access correctness, overflow page round-trips (8KB-100KB),
and free-list leak detection with interleaved operations.

Closes: bd-3ta.5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3ta.2","title":"S3-FIFO buffer pool eviction","description":"S3-FIFO buffer pool eviction (§15.1): Three-queue FIFO eviction (small/main/ghost). Lock-free queue implementation. Adaptive sizing between queues. Replaces traditional LRU/clock. Tracing: span 'buffer_evict' with fields queue(small/main/ghost), evicted_page, hit_rate_1min. Log level: DEBUG eviction decisions, INFO periodic hit rate. Metric: fsqlite_buffer_hit_ratio gauge, fsqlite_buffer_evictions_total counter by queue, fsqlite_buffer_pool_size_pages gauge.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Hit rate >= LRU on all YCSB workloads. 2. Lock-free queue operations. 3. Adaptive queue sizing converges within 1000 accesses. 4. Memory bounded by configured pool size.  5. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script validates integrated behavior with replay instructions and artifact capture. 7.  {"id":"bd-3ta.3","title":"LeanStore pointer swizzling for buffer hot-path","description":"LeanStore pointer swizzling (§15.3): In-memory page references use direct pointers instead of page IDs. Swizzle on page load, unswizzle on eviction. Eliminates page-table indirection on hot path. Tracing: span 'swizzle' with fields page_id, swizzled_in, unswizzled_out. Log level: TRACE swizzle events, DEBUG periodic swizzle ratio. Metric: fsqlite_swizzle_ratio gauge, fsqlite_swizzle_faults_total counter.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Pointer swizzling eliminates page-table lookup on hot path. 2. 2-5x improvement on random point lookups. 3. Correct under concurrent access + eviction. 4. Unswizzle on eviction is safe.  5. Comprehensive unit/property tests with deterministic seeds and invariant checks are required for this bead. 6. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script validates integrated behavior with replay instructions and artifact capture. 7. Structured logging/metrics must emit trace/run/scenario identifiers, timing, and actionable failure context, and CI must validate schema conformance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownCliff","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:55.570092334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:39:18.247096760Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:39:18.247076452Z","close_reason":"SwizzleRegistry + metrics + 13 tests, all gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","alien-pass-2026-02-13","btree","ev-gated","frankensqlite","impl"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ta.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3ta","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ta.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3ta.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3050,"issue_id":"bd-3ta.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation: LeanStore Pointer Swizzling for Buffer Hot-Path (BrownCliff)

### Summary
Added SwizzleRegistry for page-level swizzle state tracking, swizzle metrics
(ratio gauge, fault counter, in/out counters) with tracing spans, and
comprehensive tests. The registry coordinates with the instrumentation layer
to emit real-time observability data for the HOT/COOLING/COLD protocol.

### Changes

**`crates/fsqlite-btree/src/instrumentation.rs`**
- Added 4 new atomic counters: `SWIZZLE_RATIO_GAUGE`, `SWIZZLE_FAULTS_TOTAL`,
  `SWIZZLE_IN_TOTAL`, `SWIZZLE_OUT_TOTAL`
- Extended `BtreeMetricsSnapshot` with `fsqlite_swizzle_ratio`,
  `fsqlite_swizzle_faults_total`, `fsqlite_swizzle_in_total`,
  `fsqlite_swizzle_out_total`
- Added recording functions with tracing spans: `record_swizzle_in()`,
  `record_swizzle_out()`, `record_swizzle_fault()`, `set_swizzle_ratio()`
- Updated `btree_metrics_snapshot()` and `reset_btree_metrics()`

**`crates/fsqlite-btree/src/swizzle.rs`**
- Added `SwizzleRegistry` struct with `Mutex<HashMap<u64, SwizzleEntry>>`
- `SwizzleEntry` tracks temperature, swizzled flag, frame_addr per page
- Methods: `register_page()`, `try_swizzle()`, `try_unswizzle()`,
  `is_swizzled()`, `frame_addr()`, `tracked_count()`, `swizzled_count()`,
  `update_ratio()`
- 13 new tests: registry CRUD, double-swizzle rejection, unregistered page
  handling, idempotent registration, swizzle/unswizzle cycles, ratio updates,
  default impl, metrics integration

### Test Results
- 163 btree tests passing (13 new registry + metrics tests)
- All quality gates green: cargo check, clippy (pedantic+nursery), fmt

 {"id":"bd-3ta.4","title":"Bε-tree write-optimized index option","description":"Bepsilon-tree option (§15.2): Write-optimized tree with message buffers at interior nodes. Batch small writes, flush down on buffer overflow. Configurable epsilon parameter for read/write tradeoff. For write-heavy workloads. Tracing: span 'be_flush' with fields node_id, messages_flushed, cascade_depth. Log level: DEBUG flush events, INFO periodic write amplification stats. Metric: fsqlite_betree_write_amplification gauge, fsqlite_betree_buffer_utilization gauge.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Bε-tree write throughput > 2x B-tree for sequential inserts. 2. Read amplification bounded by epsilon parameter. 3. Flush cascade correct under concurrent reads. 4. Configurable epsilon with clear performance tradeoff documentation.  8. Unit/property test coverage for this bead is mandatory, including deterministic seeds and explicit invariant checks. 9. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script must validate integrated behavior with replay instructions and archived artifacts. 10. ### Summary
Added 21 comprehensive tests covering B-tree sorted order/balance after
random insert/delete, S3-FIFO cache invariants, SwizzleRegistry correctness
under concurrent access, overflow page round-trips, and free-list leak
detection.

### Changes

**`crates/fsqlite-btree/src/btree_invariant_tests.rs`** (NEW)
- 21 tests across 5 categories:

**B-tree Sorted Order & Balance (5 tests):**
- `btree_invariant_sequential_insert_1000`: forward + reverse scan after 1K inserts
- `btree_invariant_reverse_insert_1000`: reverse-order insertion, verify sorted
- `btree_invariant_insert_delete_interleaved`: 500 insert, delete odds, insert more
- `btree_invariant_delete_all_then_reinsert`: empty tree, reinsert different rows
- `btree_invariant_random_insert_delete` (proptest, 50 cases): random ops up to 2K

**S3-FIFO Cache (3 tests):**
- `s3fifo_hit_rate_scan_heavy_workload`: sequential scan + hot set, hit rate vs LRU
- `s3fifo_capacity_invariant_never_exceeded`: resident count never exceeds capacity
- `s3fifo_ghost_admission_tracks_evicted`: evicted pages appear in ghost queue

**Swizzle Registry (6 tests):**
- `swizzle_registry_basic_lifecycle`: register/swizzle/unswizzle 100 pages
- `swizzle_registry_double_swizzle_rejected`: second swizzle with different addr fails
- `swizzle_registry_unswizzle_unregistered`: unregistered page returns false
- `swizzle_ptr_state_transitions`: SwizzlePtr atomic swizzle/unswizzle
- `page_temperature_valid_transitions`: Cold→Hot→Cooling→Cold cycle
- `page_temperature_invalid_transition`: Cold→Cooling rejected
- `swizzle_registry_concurrent_access`: 4 threads, 1000 pages, swizzle/unswizzle

**Overflow Pages (3 tests):**
- `overflow_large_payload_round_trip`: 8KB payload
- `overflow_multiple_large_payloads`: 10 payloads of 6.5-11KB
- `overflow_very_large_payload`: 100KB payload spanning many overflow pages

**Free-list (4 tests):**
- `freelist_no_page_leaks_insert_delete_all`: 500 insert, delete all, verify empty
- `freelist_interleaved_insert_delete_bounded_growth`: insert/delete/insert cycles
- `freelist_random_ops_no_corruption` (proptest, 20 cases): random ops verify sorted order + payload integrity

**`crates/fsqlite-btree/src/lib.rs`**
- Added `#[cfg(test)] mod btree_invariant_tests;`

### Acceptance Criteria Status
- [x] B-tree invariants: sorted order after random insert/delete
- [x] S3-FIFO: hit rate comparison, capacity invariant, ghost tracking
- [x] Pointer swizzling: correctness under concurrent access
- [x] Overflow pages: large payloads round-trip correctly
- [x] Free-list: no page leaks after mixed insert/delete

### Test Results
- 184 btree tests passing (21 new, 163 existing)
- All quality gates green: cargo check, clippy (pedantic+nursery), fmt

 {"id":"bd-3ta.6","title":"Learned index structures for static lookup tables","description":"Learned Index Structures (§8.4): PLA-based learned indexes for static/slowly-changing lookup tables. Model replaces upper levels of B-tree with linear regression segments. Fallback to traditional B-tree for dynamic data. Tracing: span 'learned_lookup' with fields model_segments, prediction_error, fallback_used. Log level: DEBUG model prediction, INFO model retrain triggers. Metric: fsqlite_learned_index_predictions_total counter, fsqlite_learned_index_fallback_ratio gauge.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1.  {"id":"bd-3ta.7","title":"Swiss Tables (SIMD hash map) for index lookups","description":"Swiss Tables (§7.7): SIMD-accelerated hash map for internal index lookups (hash joins, GROUP BY, DISTINCT). Uses SSE2/AVX2 control byte probing for cache-line parallel lookup. Drop-in replacement for std::collections::HashMap. Tracing: span 'hash_probe' with fields probes, items, load_factor. Log level: TRACE per-probe. Metric: fsqlite_swiss_table_probes_total counter, fsqlite_swiss_table_load_factor gauge.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Swiss Table lookup 2x faster than std HashMap for 8-byte keys. 2. SSE2/AVX2 probe path verified. 3. Load factor maintained < 87.5%. 4. Drop-in replacement API.  5. Comprehensive unit/property tests with deterministic seeds and invariant checks are required for this bead. 6. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script validates integrated behavior with replay instructions and artifact capture. 7. Structured logging/metrics must emit trace/run/scenario identifiers, timing, and actionable failure context, and CI must validate schema conformance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownCliff","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:10.136704185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:09:58.025583245Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:09:58.025562596Z","close_reason":"SwissIndex integrated: HashMap replaced in VdbeEngine+MemDatabase, 2 integration tests, 264 VDBE tests green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","alien-pass-2026-02-13","btree","ev-gated","frankensqlite","impl"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ta.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3ta","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ta.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3ta.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3047,"issue_id":"bd-3ta.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation: Swiss Tables (SIMD hash map) for Index Lookups (BrownCliff)

### Summary
Replaced `std::collections::HashMap` with the existing `SwissIndex` wrapper (backed by
`hashbrown::HashMap` for SIMD-accelerated probing) in VdbeEngine and MemDatabase.
Extended the `SwissIndex` API to support all usage patterns found in the engine.

### Changes

**`crates/fsqlite-btree/src/swiss_index.rs`**
- Added `get_mut()` method for mutable value access with probe tracking
- Added `entry_or_insert_with()` — wraps hashbrown's entry API while
  recording probe metrics (avoids borrow-checker issues with raw entry())
- Added `values()` method for iterating values
- Added `std::ops::Index<&Q>` trait implementation for indexing syntax
- Added `IntoIterator` impls for `&SwissIndex` and `&mut SwissIndex`
- Added `FromIterator` impl

**`crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs`**
- Replaced `use std::collections::HashMap` with `use fsqlite_btree::swiss_index::SwissIndex`
- Changed 5 struct fields from `HashMap<i32, _>` to `SwissIndex<i32, _>`:
  - `VdbeEngine::cursors`, `sorters`, `storage_cursors`, `aggregates`
  - `MemDatabase::tables`
- Converted all `HashMap::new()` → `SwissIndex::new()`
- Converted `.entry(k).or_insert_with(f)` → `.entry_or_insert_with(k, f)`
- 2 new integration tests:
  - `test_swiss_index_metrics_emitted_on_cursor_ops` — verifies probe metrics
    fire on create_table and get_table_mut operations
  - `test_swiss_index_replaces_hashmap_in_engine` — smoke test verifying engine
    runs programs with SwissIndex maps

### Test Results
- 264 VDBE tests passing (2 new, 262 existing)
- 2 btree SwissIndex unit tests passing
- All quality gates green: cargo check, fmt
","created_at":"2026-02-18T00:09:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3tc7","title":"5F.4: Crash recovery and WAL replay correctness test","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:22:15.054120637Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:46:13.633191799Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:46:13.633169637Z","close_reason":"Implemented crash recovery/WAL replay correctness tests via subprocess abort helpers in crates/fsqlite-e2e/tests/recovery_crash_wal_replay.rs; validated targeted test pass; cargo check + fmt pass; full clippy currently blocked by existing lints in crates/fsqlite-e2e/tests/e2e_storage_stack.rs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3tc7","depends_on_id":"bd-mxx9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3tc7","depends_on_id":"bd-ujkm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2659,"issue_id":"bd-3tc7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# 5F.4: Crash recovery and WAL replay correctness test

## What

WAL mode provides crash recovery: if the process crashes mid-write, the WAL file contains
enough information to replay committed transactions and discard uncommitted ones. This test
simulates crashes at various points and verifies recovery produces correct state.

## Why this matters

Without crash recovery testing, we can't claim durability. The WAL replay path is exercised
rarely in normal operation (only after unclean shutdown), making it a prime candidate for
latent bugs.

## Crash simulation approach

We can't actually crash the process mid-write in a test. Instead:

1. **Controlled abort**: Write data, call `std::process::abort()` in a subprocess, then
   recover in the parent process. Use `std::process::Command` to spawn a child that:
   - Opens database, writes data, commits some transactions
   - At a specified point, calls `abort()` (skipping Drop impls, leaving WAL dirty)
   - Parent then opens the same database and verifies recovery

2. **Manual WAL manipulation**: Write valid WAL frames, then truncate the WAL at specific
   points to simulate partial writes. Open the database and verify recovery.

3. **fsync simulation**: Use VFS hooks (if available) to skip fsync and simulate power loss.

## Test plan

### Test 1: Committed transactions survive crash

```rust
#[test]
fn test_committed_txn_survives_crash() {
    let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
    let path = dir.path().join("crash1.db");

    // Phase 1: Write and commit in subprocess
    let output = Command::new(env::current_exe().unwrap())
        .arg("--crash-helper")
        .arg("committed_crash")
        .arg(path.to_str().unwrap())
        .output()
        .unwrap();
    // Helper: CREATE TABLE, INSERT 100 rows, COMMIT, then abort()
    // WAL contains committed frames

    log!("[CRASH][test=committed_survives][step=child_exit] status={}", output.status);

    // Phase 2: Recovery in parent
    let conn = Connection::open(path.to_str().unwrap()).unwrap();
    // WAL replay should recover the committed transaction
    let count = conn.query_value::<i64>("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t").unwrap();
    assert_eq!(count, 100);
    log!("[CRASH][test=committed_survives][step=recovery] rows={count} ✓");
}
```

### Test 2: Uncommitted transaction discarded after crash

```rust
#[test]
fn test_uncommitted_txn_discarded_after_crash() {
    let dir = tempdir().unwrap();
    let path = dir.path().join("crash2.db");

    // Phase 1: Committed data
    {
        let conn = Connection::open(path.to_str().unwrap()).unwrap();
        conn.execute("CREATE TABLE t(x INTEGER)").unwrap();
        for i in 0..50 {
            conn.execute(&format!("INSERT INTO t VALUES ({i})")).unwrap();
        }
        conn.close().unwrap();
    }

    // Phase 2: Uncommitted data + crash in subprocess
    // Helper: BEGIN, INSERT 50 more rows, abort() WITHOUT COMMIT
    let output = Command::new(env::current_exe().unwrap())
        .arg("--crash-helper")
        .arg("uncommitted_crash")
        .arg(path.to_str().unwrap())
        .output()
        .unwrap();

    log!("[CRASH][test=uncommitted_discarded][step=child_exit] status={}", output.status);

    // Phase 3: Recovery
    let conn = Connection::open(path.to_str().unwrap()).unwrap();
    let count = conn.query_value::<i64>("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t").unwrap();
    assert_eq!(count, 50); // Only the first 50 committed rows
    log!("[CRASH][test=uncommitted_discarded][step=recovery] rows={count} ✓ (expected 50)");
}
```

### Test 3: WAL with multiple committed transactions

```rust
// Helper writes: BEGIN, INSERT 10, COMMIT, BEGIN, INSERT 10, COMMIT, BEGIN, INSERT 10, abort()
// Recovery should have 20 rows (two committed txns), not 30
```

### Test 4: Crash during checkpoint

```rust
// Helper: Write data, begin checkpoint, abort() mid-checkpoint
// Recovery: database should be consistent (either pre-checkpoint or post-checkpoint state)
```

### Test 5: WAL header corruption recovery

```rust
// Manually corrupt WAL header salt values
// Open database: should either recover gracefully or report clean error
// Must NOT silently use corrupted data
```

## Structured logging

```
[CRASH][test=NAME][step=STEP] message | key=value
```

## Subprocess crash helper

Implement as a test binary helper:
```rust
fn crash_helper_committed(path: &str) {
    let conn = Connection::open(path).unwrap();
    conn.execute("CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS t(x INTEGER)").unwrap();
    for i in 0..100 {
        conn.execute(&format!("INSERT INTO t VALUES ({i})")).unwrap();
    }
    // COMMIT happened via autocommit
    // Now abort without clean shutdown
    std::process::abort();
}
```

## Tests

- `test_committed_txn_survives_crash`: Committed data visible after recovery
- `test_uncommitted_txn_discarded`: Uncommitted data gone after recovery
- `test_multiple_committed_txns_crash`: Only committed txns survive
- `test_crash_during_checkpoint`: Database consistent after checkpoint crash
- `test_wal_header_corruption`: Graceful handling of corrupted WAL
